mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: GREEN: R - Reduce energy consumption, E - E-waste management, E - Efficient resources, N - Net-zero targets

acronyms.usage: Discarded electronic devices and components that contain toxic materials
Gases like CO2 and methane that trap heat in the atmosphere contributing to climate change
Network of connected devices that share data, key to Industry 4.0
Machines that simulate human intelligence, central to Fourth Industrial Revolution
Technology infrastructure enabling digital transformation and Green IT solutions

visualTips.visualization: Draw a horizontal timeline with four major phases marked at 1760, 1870, 1950, and 2010, with key inventions written above each period
A pyramid showing: Base - Resource Extraction, Middle - Manufacturing Pollution, Top - E-waste and Carbon Emissions, with sustainability as the capstone
A circular arrows diagram showing: Production → Consumption → Recycling → Reuse → Production (continuous loop)
Three concentric circles: Inner - Energy Efficiency, Middle - Virtualization/Cloud, Outer - Sustainable Lifecycle Management

analogies.analogy: Just as an engine converts fuel into motion, industrialization converts natural resources into products, but needs to be 'tuned' for sustainability
Like a person on a treadmill who walks faster to burn same calories, efficiency gains make us consume more, not less
In a natural ecosystem, nothing is wasted - nutrients cycle through. Circular Economy mimics this zero-waste principle
Just as a body's nervous system monitors and adjusts functions, sensors and AI in smart factories monitor and optimize production in real-time
